sample_rows.Behaviour: 13pm - 14pm, Mathew went out for a drive with staff. When asked to get ready for going out, he appeared elated in mood and was excitedly jumping around. He was presenting as settled on the back sit of his car as the drive progress. Later on Mathew was presenting unstable in mood as he was screaming and slapping himself on the face. He appeared to be in distress . He was offered crisps which he ate in a hurry and a drink, which he spilled. Re-assurance was given to MN by staff. Also, staff  put the air condition on and rolled down the windows in order to make sure he get enough ventilation in order to regulate his body temperature. He was unsettled for about 7 minutes, after which he eventually settled and started smiling again.

sample_rows.Summary:
Tuesday 6th April 2021

I arrived at sw house at 5.45pm and had a good handover from his pa Karen, sw was in a reasonable good mood, when Karen left he talked about his old friends from school, he said his grandfather was getting better and that he loved his mum.  He spoke about his neighbour Sonia, but I changed the subject and asked if he wanted a cup of tea. The tv was on and he was watching the soaps, he was talking about his walk and seemed excited about doing it, I asked if he was okay with going for a walk at the weekend and he said yes. I asked if he wanted his tea yet and he said no, i'll tell you when i'm ready.  At 10.15pm , he decided he wanted his tea and I made him a cheese and ham omelette with a cup of tea and gave him his medication. Not long after I changed him into his pyjamas, as I was pulling up his clothes and side onto him, without any warning, he stuck his tongue into my right ear, I pulled away quickly and told him not to do that. I got him back into the front room and he started talking about an old school friend called Lynne who used to eat her own faeces, he started talking again about pads and nappies so I changed the subject to animals and avon delivery which worked. He stayed up talking and watching tv until 00.40am and apologised for keeping me up late, I secured the house and went to the staff room around 1am.

Wednesday 7th April 2021

I got out of bed at 7am to go for a wash and shave and found sw sat on the toilet already awake. I said good morning and asked if he wanted a cup of tea, he replied yes and said I'M WARNING YOU DEL, DO NOT TALK TO THE NEIGHBOUR NEXT DOOR and made a hand gesture to me by running his finger across his throat and then pointing to me, implying that he would do that to me if I spoke to the neighbour, I ignored that and told him his cup of tea was ready and took him into the front room. I then went into the staff room to get changed, when i came back into the front room he asked for his medication. His pa Ron arrived for handover at 9am, and sw informed Ron not to talk to the neighbour also. sw was in a good mood when I left.

Friday 9th April 2021

i took over my shift and handover at 2pm from Sylla SW was watching the news about Prince Phillips death Myself and SW watched this sad event all day at 18.30 SW decided to have early tea due to the news on TV he was in a somber mood but stayed happy within himself.  SW started to talk about the previous week and apologised for his mood swings towards all the staff that worked with him and continued to talk about his charity walk and sponsers and looking forward to it. At 9.40 SW was medicated and carried on watching the TV until 11.40 when he went to bed in a good mood I secured the house and made my way to the staff room Derek McCallister Support Worker.

sample_rows.IncidentDetail: At 22.01 without warning, Steven started shouting, screaming and swearing at high volume, he said he wished his mother and brother James and James's dog Bhodi were dead and also mentioned a woman called Donna Brady who is a friend of his mothers and he hates her very much. I diverted him by changing the subject and talking about our day out at Chester zoo and Walton Hall Gardens, I also told him to drink plenty of water. I could see he was calming down, so I knelt down in front of him and we played the number game which made him smile and his left hand came completely  down, I asked him how he was feeling now and he said better, there was no more discussion about his family, I made him a cup of tea and we resumed watching tv until it was his bed time which was just after midnight.

sample_rows.Title: Choice and Control
sample_rows.Outcome: To enable Anthony to express his choices and make simple decisions that are important to him.   Any decisions made for Anthony are in his best interests.   To support Anthony as an individual
sample_rows.WhatCanIdo: Anthony can understand simple information & instructions, Anthony communicates verbally and will also use some gestures to communicate.    Anthony will greet staff or visitors by saying “Hello Darling” or “No not you again, what are you doing here”.   Anthony will use gestures such as pointing when he is unable to form the words to express his needs or what it is he wants at the time. Anthony’s body language can also inform staff of his mood at the time, for example, if he seems fidgety or slapping his hand on the table, he may be agitated or bored. Anthony can make decisions about his meals, what he would like to wear, if he needs any personal care and what he would like to do as an activity.
sample_rows.SupportWith: Choice and Control:   Anthony can inform staff of some of his needs by verbal communication, he does however use set words and phrases so can respond with yes or no when the opposite is a true account at that time.  Anthony will tell staff if he does not want to something or does not want to engage, he does not however understand the rationale behind things as he is unable to process more complex information. Anthony can show you what he wants or likes by choosing and pointing to an item such as clothing. Anthony has been assessed as lacking capacity in certain areas because of a learning disability as this affects his cognitive function and ability to learn. Anthony should always be offered as much choice as possible and given the time to make a choice.  Anthony’s support staff will make daily decisions that reflect his needs, his identity and value his uniqueness. Always give Anthony the chance to make his own decisions for everyday stuff.  Anthony is an adult at risk and could be vulnerable to abuse, he could be at risk of abuse: financial, physical, sexual, emotional, organisational, and discriminatory. Anthony can make most non-complex decisions himself but may need staff to explain things for him.   Communication:   Anthony communicates using the English Language and has set words in his vocabulary that he is used to using. Anthony will often say NO, for example: if you ask him if he has been out, he can say No when he has just arrived back home from an activity. Anthony has become used to set phrases and will use these in his daily life, for areas such as greetings.    Anthony will greet staff or visitors by saying “Hello Darling” or “No not you again, what are you doing here”.   Anthony will use gestures such as pointing when he is unable to form the words to express his needs or what it is he wants at the time. Anthony’s body language can also inform staff of his mood at the time for e.g. If Anthony becomes fidgety, he is bored and eager to do something different or go for a drive, it may also mean that his 1-1 staff are talking to someone else, and he wants attention from them.   Anthony should be always communicated with, so he is aware of what is happening in his home, his environment and that he feels important and valued. Anthony is quite shy and can become embarrassed easy. He loves attention and staff saying nice things to him or about him. Anthony is also sensitive to anyone speaking about him to other staff. He will shout out “not me, I didn’t do it”.  Staff are to be mindful of this and remember to consider his feelings and protect his dignity. When communicating with Anthony it is important to use clear sentences and give him time process the information. Anthony is prescribed glasses for distance. He does not always wear these and should be encouraged by staff daily, especially if he is to complete a task where these would be helpful. Optician prescription states that if he wears these for tasks, so he is comfortable, then he does not have to wear them all the time. Anthony can hear quite well.   Reading and Writing:   Anthony is unable to read and write, he can copy write a name on a colouring he has done for someone. Anthony’s correspondence is dealt with by staff and any relating to finances or property are dealt with by his court appointed deputy from the Local authority. Anthony has been prescribed glasses and will wear these with prompts from staff when he is completing a task that requires concentration. He keeps these in a case in his room. He can get these himself if prompted from staff.   Concentration:   Anthony has limited ability to concentrate for long periods of time. Anthony may start an activity and within 10-15 minutes decide to stop the activity. Anthony does not appear to spend time resting or relaxing and will “wander” around his home environment. Anthony will tend to gravitate towards staff rather than his housemates/peers. Anthony is encouraged to take some time to rest, relax and energise but will become fidgety and start moving around before he returns to walking around the house. If Anthony is tired of doing something, he will get up and walk away or start to tap on the nearest surface or become fidgety. Anthony will usually sit in the lounge of an evening to watch some T.V before supper. Staff are to join him in this. He may not watch too much of the television but enjoys sitting there anyway.   Capacity and Decision Making:   Anthony has been assessed to see whether he has capacity to make decisions about where he lives and who provides his care. It was decided that he does not have capacity to make such decisions and a best interest meeting was held to choose the least restrictive option.  It was decided that living at Oldfield Drive and having his care provided by BNR is in his best interests, it is the least restrictive and is proportionate to meet his needs. An email has been sent to Leanne Edmunds to enquire whether a DOLS application would be necessary, and we are waiting a response. Anthony is unable to leave his home safely and independently, his care package is tailored to enable him to access the community and prevent isolation. Anthony has a care package which provides 15hrs of direct 1:1 support each day. Anthony is to be given the chance to make all day-to-day non-complex decisions with the support from staff. Anthony seems quite happy to reside at Oldfield and has not caused any concern regarding to leave the house without supervision. If Anthony does wish to leave the house, staff are to escort him, ask him to return to ensure safety and contact registered manager for further advice.
sample_rows.OutcomeAssociatedWith: Support to engage in activities of interest and choice and access the community.   Anthony to make as many decisions as possible in is daily life and these to be respected.   Support to meet Anthony’s needs effectively.   Anthony’s staff team to review needs and ensure Anthony is up to date with all appointments    DOLS enquiry to be followed up.
sample_rows.Risk: Lack of Capacity & decision-making skill   Deprivation of Liberty   Communication difficulties and expressing need.   Risk of abuse   Isolation

sample_rows.SupportWith: Current Health Conditions:      Anthony is living with a severe learning disability, Epilepsy, Curvature of the spine and a heart murmur.         Historic Health Conditions:    Anthony has previously had Gout, Whooping cough at Birth, he has had surgery for a detached retina of his right eye, oedema to legs, constipation and was also diagnosed with Dysphagia.         Learning Disability:   Anthony is diagnosed with a severe learning disability, which affects his ability to communicate, process information and cognitive development. Due to this he can struggle to express his needs, and this creates heightened anxiety. Anthony’s learning disability also affects his ability to make decisions and understand consequences.   Epilepsy:   Anthony is diagnosed with Epilepsy, when he has had seizures, these are of the Tonic-Clonic type. Anthony has been seizure free for approximately 3 years however mum informed us that he used to have seizures in his sleep and have urine incontinence. Anthony is reviewed by Dr.Sekhar at The Walton Neurology Centre. Anthony’s Epilepsy is managed by prescribed anti-convulsant medication (Epilim chrono) and he is compliant with this. Anthony had a letter from DR Sekhar on 16/02/2022 stating because Anthony has had no seizures during the last 12 months his management would not be changed and a follow up appointment would be booked in 12 months' time, 2023. 14.5.23, a call from Walton has been arranged to review. Stress   Missed dose of anti-epileptic medication (epilum)   Late nights and lack of sleep   Illness   Missed meals   Flickering or flashing lights      All BNR staff complete a mandatory epilepsy E-learning that is refreshed annually     It is important that staff are aware that Anthony’s medication can cause him to be extra sensitive to the sun, Anthony should be encouraged and supported to apply a high SPF sunscreen, and sit in the shade where possible. Anthony does not currently have any rescue medications in the event of a seizure due to him being seizure free for 3+ years      Heart Murmur:   Anthony is under the care of his GP and has an ECG as part of an annual health check to monitor his heart. Anthony will walk leaning back and will often stop and hold on to railings, items that are immediately there for him to take a rest. Anthony was referred by G.P to physiotherapist Sain Dillon. Anthony now has a rollator with a seat attached for rest, he uses this outdoors only so he can continue to enjoy accessing the community. He especially likes cafes and shops. This is kept in his car. Staff are to supervise Anthony and ensure he has applied the brakes on/off when needed. He manages without this indoors and can climb stairs without any assistane however Anthony is to be always supervised. Anthony will use his toilet in in his bedroom and prefers to be on his own so staff are to wait outside for him and check if he is ok after a few minutes. Anthony has leaned on a chair in the past and lost his balance. This caused him to fall so staff are to make sure the area is clutter free so he can walk around the house independently. Anthony was eating food very quickly which could have led to choking and breathing difficulties. Anthony now eats a meal at a pace that does not place him at risk, and he does enjoy a varied diet. Anthony no longer has support from SALT team as he was discharged. It is important for staff to be aware in case he displays any signs of choking, swallowing problems where he may require additional medical advice and intervention.   The speech and language therapy report can be found at the back of Anthony’s support file and at the back of his health and wellbeing file    Constipation: Anthony has previously suffered with constipation and required medical intervention and prescription laxatives. Anthony has a varied diet with plenty of fruit and vegetables and fluids. Anthony has poor mobility and can sometimes show signs of discomfort but he is encouraged to move about the house, and to and from car as a form of exercise, if he is comfortable. It is important for staff to be aware of the importance of a balanced diet, good fluid intake and a little exercise as this can have an impact on his physical health such as: impaction, bowel leakage and may affect his mood and behaviours if not managed. Staff to report any concerns to Team leader on duty.   Oral Health Care:    Anthony requires staff encouragement and support to attend to his oral care twice a day. Anthony has been advised from the bowel and bladder service to drink decaf tea so not to have his bladder overstimulated and trigger any urgent need to pass urine. Anthony may have an ‘accident’ due to not getting to the toilet on time which may then trigger a distressed reaction. There are currently no concerns with Anthony’s diet or weight. Anthony needs staff who know him well to go to appointments, keep him focused so that he can have blood tests done. Anthony will take the medicine from staff and put in his mouth with staff supervision. He will drink water from a beaker independently also. Staff will then record this on the Emar system using the tablet provided. Any concerns are to be reported to team leader on duty. Anthony is prescribed PRN paracetamol and is offered this daily to aid back pain and discomfort. It has been requested that this medicine is prescribed regular as this seems to benefit Anthony. Medication:   Dose:   Reason prescribed/use:   Epilim Chrono   500mg – one tablet twice a day   Anti-Convulsant   Diazepam    2mg- one tablet three times a day   anxiety   Colecalciferol 800 unit/calcium phosphate 3.1g oral powder sachets   One sachet daily   Vitamin D supplement   Sertraline    100mg at 0800	   Depression   Paracetamol   500mg – 2 tablets to be taken 3 times a day Morning, afternoon and evening.   Mild to moderate pain. This will also help with a fever.      Staff who are fully trained and competent to administer medications are the only people who can give Anthony his medication.    ++All staff to read and sign medication file++   Sleep Pattern:   Anthony will usually go to bed between 9 & 10pm, he will at times wake through the night and should be encouraged to return to bed. Anthony is an early riser and will wake any time from approximately 5am in the summer months.    Anthony will usually have his supper after his evening medication. He usually has tea and toast or whatever he fancies. He will sit at the kitchen table with his 1-1 staff then he will take himself off to bed. He likes his staff to wish him goodnight and maybe tell him what the plans are for him tomorrow. This makes him smile if he has something nice to look forward to. Anthony may wake in the night to use the toilet. He may sometimes sleep through, and his bed will be wet with urine. He does have a Kylie sheet prescribed from the bowel and bladder service, this will hold some fluid however, Anthony will be uncomfortable so will want this changing. He will get embarrassed by this so staff are to be as discreet as possible when taking this to the laundry room. Anthony will then be offered a shower and personal care.      Continence:    Anthony has had periods of incontinence of both urine and faeces. Anthony will require the occasional prompt or reminder to use the toilet, he may hold his clothing and cross his legs suggesting he needs to urinate. Anthony when prompted will use his bathroom. He likes to use the toilet privately so staff are to ask/check if he is clean and comfortable to protect his dignity. Anthony may sometimes use all the toilet roll and may leave his toilet in a bit of a mess so staff are to check this and ensure area is clean and fit for use. Staff are to prompt Anthony to wash his hands after using the toilet. Anthony regularly has urine incontinence of a night and will need support from staff to assist with personal care and to put fresh linen on his bed. He has been prescribed Kylie sheets, from the bowel and bladder service, which go over the sheet. This will absorb some of the urine and help prevent a full bed change in the night so Anthony is not disturbed too much. He has a mattress protector on. Anthony will take his own soiled linen to the laundry room but staff are to be very discreet about this as Anthony gets embarrassed and upset by this. He has a laundry basket in his bedroom and Anthony will take his dirty washing down stairs later, with staff. Night staff are to hand anything over to day staff so the bedroom is clean and tidy and presentable.   It is essential that Anthony has a varied diet and does not suffer from constipation as this can lead to serious health effects and could potentially cause bowel overflow and leakage. Bowel overflow suggests a high level of constipation and staff need to be able to document this and take appropriate action by seeking medical input. Bowel overflow looks like water and can be very malodorous (smell offensive).      Illness:   Anthony may tell staff if he develops an infection or new illness, but he may not. He may be quieter than normal, pale complexion or off his food. Staff are reassure Anthony and encourage him to seek G.P input if needed. Anthony will accept his blood pressure and temperature taken by a team leader. Anthony is anxious about hospitals and doctors so may be reluctant to tell staff if he is in any pain. Staff are to reassure Anthony, any concerns than report to team leader on duty. Anthony needs constant reassurance, however he will allow his 1-1 staff to make a call to 111 G.P out of hours if the surgery is closed. All findings should then be given to 111 or G.P for further advice. Anthony is prescribed regular paracetamol. Anthony has been fully vaccinated with the PfiZer Covid-19 Vaccine, his 1st Dose of Covid vaccine was given on 03.02.2021 & the second on 10.04.2021. Anthony has also received his booster vaccine on 05.11.2021.   Documentation:   Anthony needs support staff to ensure that he has a hospital passport and keep this updated in case he has any admissions as this informs others about his health and any reasonable adjustments required.       Anthony also has a Health Action Plan to ensure that he has access to all health services and health is a key priority.      Anthony will require his health records in his health file to be kept up to date so that there is a clear trail of which appointments he has had and any feedback or follow up actions.   ++Additional health resources which details health conditions are kept in Anthony’s health and Wellbeing file, all staff to read and sign++

sample_rows.Title: Everyday Tasks, Living safely and taking risks
sample_rows.Outcome: To maintain safety during personal care routine by preventing falls and injury    To maintain privacy and dignity   To prevent personal care deterioration    To participate in activities of daily living   To ensure that Anthony is safe in his home and community
sample_rows.WhatCanIdo: Anthony is able to carry out some everyday tasks with supervision   Anthony can participate in basic personal care.   Anthony can follow simple instructions and access the community with his 1,1 staff member.   Anthony can follow simple instructions at home in order to keep him safe.
sample_rows.SupportWith: Personal Care:   Anthony requires 1 carer to support him with his personal care needs. During his personal care routine staff support him with his personal hygiene and ensure that he can wash his body and hair. Staff to prompt Anthony in his personal care routine so he washes his hair and body with minimal support.   Anthony has an ensuite bathroom with shower, he likes to have a shower each morning. As Anthony has an en-suite it is important for staff to ensure his blinds are closed during his personal care to maintain his dignity. Anthony is unable to regulate the shower and water temperature independently, so staff need to do this for him and ensure it is safe prior to him stepping into the shower cubicle.  Water temperature should be between 39-41c.  Anthony is able to choose the clothes he wants to wear; he has a separate closet for his clothing as this has historically caused behaviours which have led Anthony to become distressed and aggressive. This closet in at the end of the landing and the key is kept in the kitchen, top draw.    Once Anthony has had his shower, he should be encouraged to dry himself properly as he would do this very quickly and then put his clothes on whilst his skin is still wet/damp.   He likes to sit on the chair in his bedroom to get dressed once dry.   Dressing:   Anthony gets dressed in his bedroom after drying in his bathroom, he can dress himself but does require support with socks and footwear. He likes to wear casual clothing, elasticated waistbands, and tops that cover his stomach. He has trainers with Velcro fastening and he can put these on himself but if he chooses a trainer or shoe with laces, he will need assistance from staff to fasten these. Anthony will choose his own clothes daily but will need a prompt from staff to advise on suitable clothing for the current weather.      Grooming   Anthony is unable to shave himself, he needs full support from staff to shave. Anthony is prone to moving frequently when shaving or lowering his head when shaving. Anthony does like to be shaven daily but does not like the actual shave part. Staff should support Anthony daily to shave, if this is not carried out his stubble becomes quite coarse and Anthony finds that shaving pulls at his skin and facial hair, causing discomfort. Anthony may say “ow” when staff begin to shave him, this is not always because it is hurting or due to discomfort, if Anthony is asked if it hurts, he will tell staff if it does. If staff hold their hand onto his head and speak to him as they do small shave strokes, this seems to help. Anthony should be encouraged to use his electric shaver daily as he does not like a wet shave. The electric shaver does not give him a close enough shave so there may be stubble visible. Anthony may also refuse a shave and this should be documented in his daily notes. Every other day, ask Anthony which staff member on duty will he accept a wet shave from and he will choose. Anthony’s brother Dave usually takes him to get his hair cut when needed but staff could also do this if needed. The address is The Dell, in Prenton and the staff know him there. Anthony allowa staff there to cut his hair, and does not need any assistance. He gets the same style each time, short round the back and sides and a bit longer on the top. Staff to give Anthony the money for this os his bank card and he will pay for this himself.       Anthony is happy to be supported by either Male or female staff with personal care. Most days Anthony will point at the staff member he wants to support him and say “you do it”.   Eating & Drinking:   Anthony can make himself and his staff a cup of tea with supervision from staff. Anthony will have meals prepared for him by staff but staff are to encourage him to help as much as possible, take his dishes to the sink, get things from the fridge etc. Anthony enjoys food and it important for him to be able to help with some basic meal preparation tasks. Anthony should always be supervised and only when he is in a positive mood. He can complete this task independently and enjoys doing it.   Anthony should regularly be encouraged to participate in preparation of meals and baking so he can feel he has achieved something and develop skills and confidence.  Anthony likes a simple diet like fish and chips, full English breakfast and roast dinners however he will try new things and enjoys most of the meals prepared daily by chore staff. Staff should ensure that Anthony has got a choice of what he eats and that he is eating healthy, is well hydrated and has a varied diet. He likes lunch out and is to be given a choice for lunch and he will sometimes pick McDonalds.    Staff to ensure that all food is within use by dates and food is safe to eat. Staff must always observe Food Hygiene procedures.    Staff should ensure that the fridge and kitchen area are always kept clean and make sure that all utensils are cleaned properly.    Anthony enjoys his food and has a good appetite; he should be monitored at meal times to ensure there are no signs of eating too quickly and to assist with cutting his food up if required. and usually have no problem eating but if staff notice any concerns, they should report it to management immediately.   Anthony eats at the dining table with his housemates and staff. Anthony uses his hands a lot when eating and will need prompting from staff to wash afterwards.   Bedtime routine:   Anthony likes to have supper and a hot drink before he goes to bed around 10pm. Anthony should be encouraged and supported to attend to personal care, brush his teeth and change into his pyjamas before bed.       Laundry and Domestic tasks:   Anthony can take his laundry to the laundry room under the supervision of staff and place this in the washing machine. Support staff will assist to do Anthony’s laundry and iron it.  Anthony has 2 separate laundry baskets as he will place clean clothes with those that need washing.   Domestic Tasks:   Anthony needs full support from staff with domestic duties however staff should involve Anthony in some of the domestic chores such as cleaning his room, making his bed, polishing and dusting. Anthony likes to sometimes wipe the table after a meal. He will take his own dishes to the sink. He needs prompting from staff as he does not always complete a task.       Vulnerability:   Anthony is a vulnerable adult and could be at risk from abuse. Anthony could be at risk of: Financial, physical, sexual, discriminatory, emotional, organisational abuse or neglect. Anthony has resided in many different services such as care homes and supported living, he has previously suffered from neglect and is unable to understand and inform anyone of abuse, this has impacted on his mental health and physical health. It is essential that Anthony is safeguarded from any potential for abuse. All staff working at BNR with Anthony will understand his needs and how to safeguard by following company policy and procedures and ensuring Anthony’s care is person centred and individual to him.   ++Staff will need to report any concerns they have to the Registered Manager/Deputy Manager/Team Leader, i.e. unfamiliar bruises, change in emotions, refusal of support/activities, change in behaviour. ++   ++All staff trained in safeguarding, whistleblowing and MCA, staff to follow all company policies and procedures to safeguard Anthony++   Fire Safety and ability to respond in an Emergency:   Anthony requires a PEEP (Personal Emergency Evacuation Plan) to be able to evacuate the building safely in the event of a fire. Staff will need to be familiar with this and practice emergency evacuations on a regular basis so that he does not become distressed when hearing the fire alarm. Staff will need to keep Anthony safe by testing all equipment and having risk assessments in place. Anthony's PEEP (personal emergency evacuation plan) is in file in grab box in kitchen pantry. On recent fire drills, Anthony left the building with staff with no concerns.   Risk and Hazards:   Anthony is unaware of risks and hazards and requires all health and safety requirements in the home to be taken care of by staff, this includes ensuring his room and immediate area is safe and free from hazards, checking water temperature before bathing, food safety and hygiene, fire safety, all essential health and safety checks and security of the building. In recent fire drills, Anthony handled the situation well and responded well to staff when being told he would need to leave the building with staff and his peers. Anthony’s 1-1 staff are to anticipate any potential risks and keep him safe.   Accessing the Local Community:   Anthony is unable to take himself out into the community safely. Anthony has a care package of 1:1 staff to access the community and ensure he does not become isolated. Anthony does not however have awareness of hazards and dangers and needs full support with areas such as road safety, to explain to him that he should stop at crossings and wait with staff.  Anthony has a motability car that his staff will drive using BNR business insurance with their full UK driving licence. The keys are kept in the top drawer in kitchen, Anthony also has a rollator kept in his boot which he uses for walking outdoors and around the shops. Staff are to check he has applied brakes when he needs to and supervise crossing roads etc. Anthony does not like to walk too far and will want to rest. Avoid too many stairs/steps if possible. Staff to carry rollator whilst Anthony uses stairs in the community. Avoid escalators, he may not use these safely.   Safety In the community:   Anthony relies on his 1-1 staff to keep him safe in the community. Anthony enjoys accessing the community; however, he can find it difficult to cope with environments where there are a lot of children who are noisy and running around. Anthony should be supported to be able to access events and venues, but staff should plan and look at off peak times and not choose events during school holidays as this could cause Anthony distress. Anthony now has his own Motability vehicle to use to access the community, this should be regularly checked for fuel prior to any journey and staff driving should be aware of parking so that Anthony is not leaving the vehicle close to a highway or busy road. Anthony may shout out and wave his arms at people if he becomes distressed. Staff are to be mindful of surroundings and reassurance given to reduce possible negative behaviour.    Anthony has not been shown to present a risk when travelling in his car and should be offered the choice to sit in the front or the back, whichever he may choose. He likes the radio on but not too high. Anthony should have car parked close to is chosen/ planned destination as his mobility is poor and he gets tired and uncomfortable if he must walk too far. He will have his rollator with him to help.   Anthony’s vehicle is solely for his use so should not be shared with other service users.   Staff should always assess Anthony’s mood before planning a long journey.   Staff should make sure that his vehicle is in good condition and good to go on the road before driving.    Anthony to wear a seat belt all the time and if there are any issues to report to management
sample_rows.OutcomeAssociatedWith: Maintain optimal personal hygiene.   Maintain dignity   Promote independence   Develop skills with meal preparation    To feel safe in his home   To be able to access the community safely   To be included within his community.   To practice fire evacuations and have up to date PEEP in place.

descriptions.AbpDynamicEntityProperties: Stores dynamic properties for entities, linking them via EntityFullName and DynamicPropertyId. Used for custom entity configurations.
descriptions.AbpDynamicEntityPropertyValues: Stores dynamic property values for entities, linked by EntityId and DynamicEntityPropertyId. Useful for querying custom entity attributes.
descriptions.AbpDynamicProperties: Stores dynamic property definitions for entities, enabling custom fields and configurations. Search by PropertyName, InputType, or DisplayName.
descriptions.AbpDynamicPropertyValues: Stores dynamic property values for tenants and dynamic properties, enabling flexible data enrichment. Query by DynamicPropertyId or TenantId.
descriptions.AbpEditions: Manages software editions like 'Standard' with unique IDs and display names. Tracks creation and modification times.
descriptions.AbpEntityChanges: Tracks entity changes (creation, modification, deletion) for business entities like ServiceUsers, recording change type, time, and entity ID for auditing and tracking.
descriptions.AbpEntityChangeSets: Tracks entity changes with user, tenant, and IP addresses. Useful for auditing and tracking operations like `ServiceUser/Create` by `UserId` and `TenantId`.
descriptions.AbpEntityPropertyChanges: Tracks changes to entity properties, including original and new values, property names, and types. Useful for auditing and tracking data modifications.
descriptions.AbpFeatures: Stores feature flags and their values for tenants and editions, enabling granular control over application functionality. Query by feature Name or Discriminator.
descriptions.AbpLanguages: Stores language definitions for applications, including display names like 'English' and 'العربية', and their corresponding codes like 'en' and 'ar'.
descriptions.AbpLanguageTexts: Stores multilingual text for application localization, keyed by `Key` and `LanguageName`. Supports `Source` and `TenantId` for context.
descriptions.AbpNotifications: Stores system notifications with details like notification name, entity type, and user IDs. Useful for tracking notification events and user engagement.
descriptions.AbpNotificationSubscriptions: Stores notification subscription preferences for users and entities, tracking notification names and target notifiers.
descriptions.AbpOrganizationUnitRoles: Links roles to organization units, tracking assignments by `RoleId` and `OrganizationUnitId` with creation timestamps.
descriptions.AbpOrganizationUnits: Stores hierarchical organization units with IDs, display names, and parent-child relationships. Tracks creation and modification times.
descriptions.AbpPermissions: Stores user and role permissions, tracking granted access to features like 'Pages.Users' for specific roles or users.
descriptions.AbpRoleClaims: Stores role-specific claim types and values, linking to AbpRoles via RoleId. Used for managing granular permissions for users.
descriptions.AbpRoles: Stores user roles like 'Admin' and 'Manager' with creation dates and display names. Used for managing user permissions and access control.
descriptions.AbpSettings: Stores application settings like email addresses and display names, with `Name` and `Value` columns.
descriptions.AbpTenantNotifications: Stores tenant notifications, including `NotificationName`, `CreationTime`, and `Data` (message content). Useful for tracking alerts and assignments.
descriptions.AbpTenants: Stores tenant information for the application, including tenant name, edition, and activity status. Tracks tenant creation and modification dates.
descriptions.AbpUserAccounts: Stores user account information, including email and username, for managing application users and their associated tenants. Tracks user creation and modification dates.
descriptions.AbpUserClaims: Stores user claim information, linking specific claim types and values to users. Useful for querying user permissions and attributes by UserId and ClaimType.
descriptions.AbpUserLoginAttempts: Tracks user login attempts, including IP address, browser info, and success/failure status. Useful for security analysis and auditing user access.
descriptions.AbpUserLogins: Stores user login provider and key details, linking to AbpUsers. Useful for tracking login methods and user authentication.
descriptions.AbpUserNotifications: Stores user notification records, linking users to tenant notifications. Tracks notification state and creation time for user engagement.
descriptions.AbpUserOrganizationUnits: Links users to organization units, tracking assignments with creation dates and user IDs.
descriptions.AbpUserRoles: Manages user-role assignments, linking users to specific roles within tenants. Tracks role assignments by UserId, RoleId, and TenantId.
descriptions.AbpUsers: Stores user account information, including email, name, and creation dates. Supports queries for user details and activity.
descriptions.AbpUserTokens: Stores user authentication tokens, linking to AbpUsers via UserId. Useful for tracking user login activity and token expiration dates.
descriptions.AbpWebhookEvents: Stores webhook event data, including webhook name and creation time, for tenant-specific event tracking.
descriptions.AbpWebhookSendAttempts: Tracks webhook send attempts for events and subscriptions, including response details and timestamps. Useful for debugging webhook delivery issues.
descriptions.AbpWebhookSubscriptions: Stores webhook subscriptions for event notifications, including webhook URI, secret, and active status. Tracks subscription creation time and associated tenant.
descriptions.BNR_ABCForm: Records ABC incident forms detailing antecedents, behaviors, and consequences for service users, linked to locations and incidents. Useful for analyzing incident trends and user behavior patterns.
descriptions.BNR_ABCFormBehaviour: Tracks ABC form behavior types (1, 2, 3) and their creation timestamps, linked to specific ABC forms. Useful for analyzing form interaction patterns over time.
descriptions.BNR_AboutMeServiceUser: Stores service user details including diagnoses, preferences, and needs for personalized care. Search by name, NHS number, or diagnoses.
descriptions.BNR_AboutMeServiceUserAcknowledgement: Tracks user acknowledgements for "About Me" service details, linking users and their details. Useful for tracking user engagement and acknowledgement status.
descriptions.BNR_AboutMeServiceUserInvolvedPerson: Tracks individuals involved with a service user, including their role and details. Useful for understanding relationships and user interactions.
descriptions.BNR_AboutMeServiceUserReviewer: Tracks reviewer details for AboutMeServiceUser, linking users and locations. Useful for analyzing reviewer activity and trends by UserDetailsId and LocationId.
descriptions.BNR_ActivityPlanner: Tracks daily activities for service users at specific locations, including activity type and time. Useful for planning and scheduling.
descriptions.BNR_ActivityPlanner_Entry: Tracks scheduled activities for service users at specific locations, including activity type, time, and completion status. Useful for querying daily schedules and activity completion rates.
descriptions.BNR_AnnualCheckMonitoring: Tracks annual checkup monitoring details, including checkup names, due dates, and review status. Useful for querying review progress and upcoming checkups.
descriptions.BNR_AnnualCheckMonitoringMaster: Tracks annual check monitoring records, linking locations, sites, and service users. Search for specific titles or by creation/deletion dates.
descriptions.BNR_Archived_VisitLog: Archived user visit logs, tracking page visits, IP addresses, and device information for user activity analysis.
descriptions.BNR_Audit: Tracks audit findings and comments from auditors and managers for specific sites and locations, including completion status and action plan details.
descriptions.BNR_AuditActionPlan: Tracks audit action plans, detailing actions, responsible persons, and due dates for audits. Search for specific actions or completion status.
descriptions.BNR_AuditMaster: Audits for BNR locations, covering staff, fire safety, and dignity. Tracks audit questions, names, and creation dates.
descriptions.BNR_AuditMasterSetting: Tracks audit master settings, including audit date and type, linked to specific locations. Useful for querying audit history by date or location.
descriptions.BNR_AuditQuestion: Audit questions and answers for controlled drug storage and register management, linked to audits. Search by question text, audit ID, or answer status.
descriptions.BNR_BodyMap: Stores body map images and application instructions for incidents, linking to locations, sites, and service users. Tracks creation and modification times.
descriptions.BNR_BowelMovementChart: Tracks patient bowel movements with date/time, stool size, and Bristol Stool Scale types. Links to master records and user details.
descriptions.BNR_BowelMovementChartMaster: Tracks bowel movement records for service users, including month/year and location. Useful for analyzing user health trends over time.
descriptions.BNR_CommunicationDictionary: Records communication details, including what was said or done, and its potential meaning. Useful for analyzing communication patterns and user actions.
descriptions.BNR_CommunicationPassport: This table stores communication preferences and needs for service users, detailing how they express themselves and prefer to be communicated with in various emotional states. It links to service users, locations, and sites.
descriptions.BNR_CommunicationPassportAcknowledgement: Tracks communication passport acknowledgments for users, linking user details to passport IDs. Useful for tracking acknowledgment status and timestamps.
descriptions.BNR_CommunicationPassportInvolvedPerson: Tracks individuals involved in communication passports, linking to service users and details. Useful for querying passport participants by name or relationship.
descriptions.BNR_CommunicationPassportReviewer: Tracks reviewers assigned to communication passports, linking users and locations. Useful for auditing passport review activity by user and date.
descriptions.BNR_ComplaintOrConcernOrComplement: Records complaints, concerns, and compliments from various complainant types, including service users and staff, with details like title, date received, and contact number.
descriptions.BNR_ComplaintOrConcernOrComplementReceiv: Tracks individuals receiving complaints, concerns, or compliments, linking staff or other persons to specific complaint IDs. Useful for analyzing complaint handling by staff (MemberOfStaffReceivingComplaintUserId) and complaint resolution timelines (CreationTime).
descriptions.BNR_ComplaintOrConcernOrComplementReport: Records complaints, concerns, and complements. Tracks acknowledgement, discussion, and resolution dates. Search for complaint status and outcomes.

Tracks investigation activities for customer complaints, concerns, and compliments, linking to reports via `ComplaintOrConcernOrComplementReportId` and recording `InvestigationDate` and `InvestigationAction`.
Tracks investigation plans for customer complaints, concerns, or compliments, linking to reports via ComplaintOrConcernOrComplementReportId. Includes InvestigationDate and InvestigationAction.
Records proposed action plans for customer complaints, concerns, or compliments. Tracks action plan details, dates, and associated reports.
descriptions.BNR_ComplaintOrConcernOrComplementReview: Tracks reviewer assignments for complaints, concerns, or complements, linking users, locations, and specific feedback items. Useful for analyzing reviewer activity and feedback resolution.
descriptions.BNR_ComplaintOrConcernOrComplementWitnes: Tracks witnesses for complaints, concerns, or complements, linking to service users or staff. Useful for investigating incident details.
descriptions.BNR_ComplaintRelatedPerson: Tracks individuals involved in complaints, linking to service users, staff, or other persons with roles and names. Useful for analyzing complaint participants and their associated complaint IDs.
descriptions.BNR_ConcernsActions: Tracks concerns and associated actions, linking them to users via CreatorUserId and LastModifierUserId. Useful for auditing concern resolution timelines.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorCommunityAccessEntr: Tracks costing calculator community access entries, linking to hour details. Useful for analyzing time-based access ratios and durations.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorHourDetail: Stores detailed daily hour breakdowns for costing calculations, including core hours, waking nights, and sleep hours, linked to costing master records. Useful for analyzing daily hour allocations and total hours per day.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorHourMaster: Stores hourly costing calculations for services, linking to locations, sites, and service users. Useful for analyzing service costs by date and user.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorHourModificationTra: Tracks modifications to costing calculator hours, linking to hour details and user information. Useful for auditing changes with `ModificationDate` and `ReasonForUpdate`.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorRatesAndPayCharges: Stores hourly charges and pay rates for core, day, waking night, and community access services, with effective dates and location IDs.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorSpotHourEntry: Stores hourly cost calculation entries with time ranges, durations, and ratios, linked to costing details. Useful for analyzing cost variations by time.
descriptions.BNR_CostingCalculatorWakingNightPerHourE: Tracks waking night duration and ratio for costing calculations, linking to hour details. Useful for analyzing night shift costs and durations.
descriptions.BNR_CostingRatesAndPayChargesModificatio: Tracks modifications to costing rates and pay charges, linking to `BNR_CostingCalculatorRatesAndPayCharges` and `BNR_UserDetails`. Records `ModificationDate` and `ReasonForUpdate`.
descriptions.BNR_Counselling: Records employee counselling discussions, including details of issues, responses, and agreed improvements. Tracks discussion dates and involved user IDs.
descriptions.BNR_Course: BNR_Course stores training course information, including course name, details, and CPD hours. Search for courses by name, type, or CPD hours.
descriptions.BNR_Course_Result: Stores course results for users, including marks obtained, pass/fail status, and submission details. Tracks course completion dates and operator assignments.
descriptions.BNR_Daily_Log_Master: Logs daily operational entries, linking to operators via OperatorId. Tracks creation and deletion timestamps, useful for auditing and activity analysis.
descriptions.BNR_Daily_Logs: Daily logs for BNR services, tracking client interactions, daily activities, and support plans. Search by `LogEntryTime`, `Summary`, `SiteId`, `ServiceUserId`.
descriptions.BNR_DailyLogDocuments: Stores daily log documents, including images and their metadata like file names, extensions, and URLs. Useful for retrieving specific log entries by date or document ID.
descriptions.BNR_DevelopmentAchievementItems: Tracks development achievements and progress for supervision support plans, detailing achievements made, comments, and development progress.
descriptions.BNR_FinanceRecord: Financial records track monetary transactions with amounts, dates, and purposes for additions or withdrawals. Query for transaction details by date or amount.
descriptions.BNR_FinanceRecordAllocationAgainstWithdr: Tracks financial allocations against withdrawals, linking to specific withdrawal records. Useful for analyzing allocation amounts and receipt numbers.
descriptions.BNR_FinanceRecordMaster: Stores financial records with location, site, and service user IDs. Tracks creation, modification, and deletion timestamps.
descriptions.BNR_FinanceRecordVerifier: Tracks finance record verification status by users, linking verifiers to specific finance records. Useful for auditing and tracking verification progress using `IsVerified` and `VerifiedDate`.
descriptions.BNR_FoodAndFluidChart: Tracks food and fluid intake for individuals, including meal types, portion sizes, and consumption notes. Useful for analyzing dietary patterns and fluid balance.
descriptions.BNR_FoodAndFluidChartMaster: Tracks food and fluid submissions for service users, linked to locations and sites. Query by submission date, service user ID, or location.
descriptions.BNR_Handover_Master: Tracks BNR operator handovers, including incident reporting, safeguarding concerns, and financial/medication checks. Useful for shift audits and incident analysis.
descriptions.BNR_HospitalPassport: Hospital passport for service users, detailing support needs, communication, and personal care. Search by ServiceUserId, LocationId, or Address.
descriptions.BNR_HospitalPassportReviewer: Tracks hospital passport reviewers, linking users and locations to hospital passports. Useful for auditing reviewer activity and passport assignments.
descriptions.BNR_HospitalPassportSupportPlans: Links hospital passports to support plans, tracking creation and modification details with user IDs and timestamps.
descriptions.BNR_HPFamilyOrCareTakerOrOtherPeople: Stores contact details for family, caretakers, or other individuals associated with a hospital passport. Search by name, relationship, or phone numbers.
descriptions.BNR_Incidents: Tracks incidents, including details, actions, and harm. Search by incident number, time, or type.
descriptions.BNR_IncidentWitness: Records witness details for incidents, linking witnesses to specific incidents. Search for witness names, roles, and incident IDs.
descriptions.BNR_Investigation: Tracks investigations into incidents, including safeguard status, CQC notifications, investigation dates, key findings, and outcomes. Useful for analyzing incident trends and consequences.
descriptions.BNR_InvestigationAcknowledgement: Tracks investigation acknowledgements by users, linking investigations to user acknowledgements with timestamps and status.
descriptions.BNR_InvestigationAndInvestigator: Tracks investigations and investigators, linking to safeguarding reports and users. Useful for querying investigation details by date or user ID.
descriptions.BNR_InvestigatorAndInvestigationComplete: Tracks investigators and investigation completion for safeguarding. Key columns: InvestigatorOrInvestigationCompletedBy, SelectedUserType, InvestigatorStaffUserId.
descriptions.BNR_Locations: Stores location details like name and description. Useful for querying locations by name or status.
descriptions.BNR_MyFamilyAndImportantPeople: Stores family and important people's contact details (Name, Relationship, Address, PhoneNumbersJson, EmailAddressesJson) for users (AboutMeServiceUserId).
descriptions.BNR_OperatorCourse: Tracks operator course assignments and bookings, linking operators and courses. Useful for querying course enrollment trends by operator ID and booking dates.
descriptions.BNR_PersonInterviewedAndNonInterviewedPe: Tracks individuals interviewed or not, linking to investigation processes and staff. Key columns: PersonInterviewed, NameOfPerson, InvestigationProcessId.
descriptions.BNR_PersonToInterviewAndSupplyStatement: Tracks individuals involved in safeguarding investigations, linking them to specific planning stages and user roles. Useful for querying interviewees or statement providers by InvestigationPlanningId or UserId.
descriptions.BNR_PositiveBehaviourInvolvedPerson: Links individuals to positive behavior support plans. Tracks plan associations by `PositiveBehaviourSupportPlanId` and `InvolvedPersonId` with creation dates.
descriptions.BNR_PositiveBehaviourSupportPlan: Positive Behaviour Support Plans detail service user needs, behaviours of concern, triggers, and interventions. Search for plans by ServiceUserId, LocationId, or dates.
descriptions.BNR_PositiveBehaviourSupportPlanAcknowle: Tracks acknowledgements for Positive Behaviour Support Plans, linking users to plans via `UserId` and `PositiveBehaviourSupportPlanId`. Records `IsAcknowledge` status.
descriptions.BNR_PositiveBehaviourSupportPlanReviewer: Links Positive Behaviour Support Plans to users and locations. Tracks plan reviews by UserDetailsId, LocationId, and PositiveBehaviourSupportPlanId.
descriptions.BNR_PositiveBehaviourSupportPlanSupporti: Stores supporting documents for Positive Behaviour Support Plans, including document names like 'Aadhar' and 'Pan', and their associated IDs.
descriptions.BNR_PreviousAnnualCheckMonitoring: Tracks past annual check monitoring records, including review dates and additional notes. Useful for analyzing review trends and monitoring history.
descriptions.BNR_ProcessAndReportInvolvedPerson: Tracks individuals involved in safeguarding investigations, detailing their role and connection to service users or staff. Useful for querying investigation involvement by staff ID or service user ID.
descriptions.BNR_ProcessAndReportTimelineOfEvent: Tracks event timelines for safeguarding investigations, linking events to specific reports via InvestigationProcessAndReportId. Useful for querying event details by EventDate.
descriptions.BNR_ProcessOfInvestigation: Tracks investigation processes, evidence collected, and anonymised statements. Key columns: InvestigationProcess, EvidenceNotCollected, CreationTime.
descriptions.BNR_ProcessOfInvestigationEvidenceCollec: Tracks evidence collected for investigations, linking evidence names and document IDs to specific investigation processes. Useful for retrieving evidence details by name or investigation ID.
descriptions.BNR_Question_Group: Stores video content associated with courses, linking to BNR_Course via CourseId. Useful for retrieving course videos by CourseId or VideoUrl.
descriptions.BNR_Question_Options: Stores answer options for questions, linking to BNR_Questions via QuestionId. Includes AnswerText and IsCorrect status.
descriptions.BNR_Question_Result: Stores student answers to course questions, linking results to courses and questions. Tracks correctness and marks awarded.
descriptions.BNR_Questions: Stores course-related questions, including title, type, and associated course ID. Useful for retrieving questions by course or title.
descriptions.BNR_RecruitmentChecklistDetails: Stores details for recruitment checklist items, linking to master checklists and user details. Tracks item status (IsChecked) and dates (CheckedDate, CreationTime).
descriptions.BNR_RecruitmentChecklistIdDocuments: Stores documents for recruitment checklists, linking to master checklists and user details. Tracks document types and check status.
descriptions.BNR_RecruitmentChecklistMaster: Tracks recruitment checklist progress for locations and users, including proposed start dates and contracted hours. Useful for monitoring recruitment timelines and completion status.
descriptions.BNR_RecruitmentChecklistReferences: Stores reference details for recruitment checklists, linking to master checklists and user details. Tracks reference request dates, receipt, and checking status.
descriptions.BNR_Restraint: Records restraint incidents, detailing antecedents, behaviors, strategies, and rationale. Search for restraint details, incident times (StartTime, EndTime), and severity (ServerityToVictim).
descriptions.BNR_RestraintHoldData: Records restraint hold details, including hold type, staff name, and restraint ID. Tracks hold events and staff actions.
descriptions.BNR_RiskAsseOtherUser: Stores details of individuals associated with a location for risk assessments, including their name, occupation, and organizational association. Useful for querying user types and associations by location ID.
descriptions.BNR_RiskAssesementDetails: Stores detailed risk assessment information, including likelihood, severity, and risk ratings before and after control measures. Key columns: Id, RiskAssessmentId, Code, Name, BeforeControlsRiskRating, WithMeaRiskRating.
descriptions.BNR_RiskAssessment: Tracks risk assessments for locations, service users, and individuals. Search by Title, Purpose, or LocationId.
descriptions.BNR_RiskAssessmentAcknowledgement: Tracks user acknowledgements for risk assessments, linking users to specific risk assessment IDs. Useful for auditing user awareness of risks.
descriptions.BNR_RiskAssessmentReviewer: Tracks users assigned to review specific risk assessments, linking users, locations, and assessment details. Useful for auditing reviewer assignments and history.
descriptions.BNR_Routine: This table stores routine support plans for service users, detailing their purpose, actions, and associated risks. It tracks plan creation, modification, and deletion with user and timestamp information.
descriptions.BNR_Safeguarding: Tracks safeguarding incidents, including incident details, reporting parties, and actions taken. Search for safeguarding numbers, incident dates, and locations.
descriptions.BNR_SafeguardingInvestigationPlanning: Safeguarding investigation planning, including purpose, terms of reference, and issues to clarify. Tracks investigation progress with dates and IDs.
descriptions.BNR_SafeguardingInvestigationPlanningSup: Stores supporting documents for safeguarding investigation plans, linking `DocumentName` and `DocumentId` to `SafeguardingInvestigationPlanningId`.
descriptions.BNR_SafeguardingInvestigationProcessAndR: Records safeguarding investigation processes and reports, including investigation details, evidence summaries, and established facts. Tracks investigation start dates and CQC notification dates.
descriptions.BNR_SafeguardingPerpetrator: Tracks perpetrators involved in safeguarding incidents, linking them to service users or staff via IDs and contact details. Useful for analyzing perpetrator trends and safeguarding case details.
descriptions.BNR_SafeguardingWitness: Records safeguarding witnesses, linking them to specific safeguarding cases. Search for witnesses by name, role, or safeguarding ID.
descriptions.BNR_SeizureLog: Log seizure events with date, start/end times, duration, location, and notes. Track customer seizure patterns.
descriptions.BNR_SeizureLogMaster: Logs seizure events by service user, location, and site, with monthly/yearly tracking. Useful for analyzing seizure trends by user and location over time.
descriptions.BNR_Service_User: Stores service user details for care recipients. Queries include user demographics like FirstName, Surname, and DateOfBirth, and admission dates.
descriptions.BNR_ServiceUserSupportPlan: Tracks service user support plans, including involvement reasons and notes. Search for support plan details by ServiceUserId or LocationId.
descriptions.BNR_ServiceUserWeightHistory: Tracks service user weight history, linking to locations and sites. Useful for analyzing weight trends over time for specific users.
descriptions.BNR_SingleAssessmentFramework: Tracks single assessment frameworks for locations and sites, indicating readiness for review and completion status. Useful for querying assessment progress by site or location ID.
descriptions.BNR_SingleAssessmentFrameworkMaster: Stores single assessment frameworks with names, questions, and month. Tracks creation and modification details.
descriptions.BNR_SingleAssessmentFrameworkQuestions: Stores questions, guidance, and evidence for single assessment frameworks. Tracks manager ratings and completion details.
descriptions.BNR_Sites: Tracks BNR sites with names and descriptions, linked to locations. Useful for site status and location queries.
descriptions.BNR_SleepChart: Tracks sleep events with date, time, and state. Links to user and master sleep chart data.
descriptions.BNR_SleepChartMaster: Stores sleep chart data for service users, linked to locations and sites. Query sleep charts by month/year (YYYY-MM-DD) and user ID.
descriptions.BNR_SupervisionSupportPlan: Tracks supervision plans for users, linking supervisors and supervisees with location and summary details. Useful for querying supervision history by user or location.
descriptions.BNR_SupportActionItems: Tracks support action items for supervision plans, including commitments, remedial actions, and due dates. Useful for querying action item status and deadlines.
descriptions.BNR_SupportPlan: BNR_SupportPlan stores service user support plans, detailing outcomes, individual capabilities, and support needs for health, safety, and daily living.
descriptions.BNR_SupportPlanAcknowledgement: Tracks service user support plan acknowledgements, linking users to plans. Useful for querying acknowledgement status by UserId or ServiceUserSupportPlanId.
descriptions.BNR_SupportPlanInvolvedPerson: Tracks individuals involved in service user support plans, linking `ServiceUserSupportPlanId` to `InvolvedPersonId`. Useful for querying support plan participants and their involvement history.
descriptions.BNR_SupportPlanReviewer: Tracks reviewer assignments for service user support plans, linking users and locations. Useful for auditing reviewer activity and support plan history.
descriptions.BNR_TicketMaster: Stores ticket master data including subject, messages, and admin/user status. Tracks ticket creation and modification times.
descriptions.BNR_TicketReplyMaster: Stores ticket replies with content, sender/receiver IDs, and timestamps. Useful for tracking customer support interactions and reply history.
descriptions.BNR_User_Sites: Links users to sites, tracking site assignments and primary status. Useful for user-site association queries.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetail_DBSTracker: Tracks DBS check details for users, including DBS number and dates. Useful for querying user DBS status and history.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetail_RightToWorkTracker: Tracks user right-to-work status with declaration, visa, and expiry dates. Essential for compliance and employment verification.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetails: Stores user details for system login, including staff and service users. Search by name, email, phone, and user type.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetailServiceUser: Links user details to service users and locations, tracking creation and modification times. Useful for querying user activity and associations by `LocationId`, `UserDetailsId`, and `ServiceUserId`.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetailSupervision: Tracks user supervision details, including supervision dates, proposed agendas, and confirmation status. Useful for analyzing supervision trends and completion rates.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetailSupervisionActionAndTarget: Tracks supervision actions and targets for user details, linking to user supervision and responsible users. Useful for querying action completion status by date.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetailSupervisionFeedback: Stores feedback messages related to user supervision, linking to user details and supervision records. Tracks feedback content, sender, and timestamps.
descriptions.BNR_UserDetailSupervisionLineManager: Tracks line manager assignments for user supervision, linking users to their managers and locations. Useful for querying manager relationships by UserDetailSupervisionId or LineManagerUserDetailId.
descriptions.BNR_VisitLog: Logs user visits to pages, tracking user IDs, IP addresses, and timestamps for audit and analysis.
descriptions.BNR_Visitor: Stores visitor contact and association details (FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone) for sites and locations, linked to user accounts.
descriptions.BNR_VisitorServiceUser: Tracks visitor service user associations, linking visitors to specific service users. Useful for querying visitor activity by ServiceUserId or VisitorId.
